meaning: n.小无花果树,枫树的一种
examples: The furniture is made of sycamore, beech, and leather.
He took out his sycamore scroll and placed it by the candle.
He destroyed their vines with hail and their sycamore-figs with sleet.
The Oct. 22 initial public offering of Sycamore Networks, an optical networking company, clinched it.
Smith is the president and chief executive of Sycamore Networks, a maker of hardware for optical-data networks.
